channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHH — 100 replies — 316.1/hr

FUCKING OIL IS $104!!!!!!!!!

61953190
this man is on suicide watch >>61950166

61953192
>>61953183

>how the fuck is it still below $200?

61953223

61956442
>>61956428
ah so thats why, is this good news?

61956463

61956476
Gey ass fuck
It shouldnt dump already

61956488

61956494

61956502

61956506

61956532

61956551

look at this shit. Oil is literally used in everything from energy, to getting the goods you buy to the store or people to there jobs

61956558
Everybody has become such a gambler that they're adding shorts left and right. Monday will end with oil at 120-130.

61956565
>>61956478
>speculation
So when they don't release it, we go back up.

61956566
>>61956532
I think they are betting on the war ending soon. In which reality do these people live?
Does everyone actually believe that the orange monkey nigger will magically end the war in two weeks?

61956635
did they released cum reserves already? why drop to 100 ?

61956651
>>61956635

61956656
>>61953183
What part of 'short term pain for long term gain' don't you retards understand? The big guy's got a plan. All this market noise is generated by libtards to make him look bad. I feel bad for you retards for thinking these squiggly lines on your screen are real and mean something.

61956675

that's what they're being told
and they will listen lol

61956683

61956915

61957087

The west has been mindbroken by peace for so long that they can't fathom a war that last multiple years anymore unless it is some cold conflict against an insurgency.
It's like how people thought WW1 was going to last a couple months because there hadn't been a major war in Europe for a hundred years aside from the Prussians sealclubbing the Austrians and French when they were weak.

61957147
>>61956463

61957315

61957348

item.title: How high will OIL go? — 38 replies — 152.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How high will OIL go?

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-03-09 12:34 UTC

OP

150?
200?

Early replies (first 3)

61950050
>>61949961
The Trump admin probably starts trying to implement price controls above $150.

61950059
>>61949961
10,000 Gold.

61950077
How will crypto perform without rate cuts in the next 3 years?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61950718
>>61950184
The chinese tech from 2004? Imagine using that
>>61949961
They sell like 85% of that oil to middlemen who then sell the oil to China so not a big deal

61950728
>>61950713
(cont.)
A study recently done by Kansas State University found:

"Longer term, the price increase could be 4.2 cents per gallon for every dollar per barrel"

$226 × $0.042 = $9.49/gallon
$243 × $0.042 = $10.20/gallon

If we take the lowest percentage (bent by the gov's inflation calculator) of 348.6% and multiply that by the lowest price per barrel in the past year of $56.52, we get $197.03/barrel translating to $8.28/gallon.

Problems with stocking up on gas right now:

The gasoline supply in the US will remain the winter formulation until the beginning of May. Winter gas has a higher Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP), meaning it evaporates more easily than summer gas. This is less conducive to long-term storage. While people often claim that the RVP is so the gas ignites easier in winter, in reality the different formulation is mostly due to cost and air pollution concerns - in the winter, you can get away with cheaper formulations because the cold temps lead to less air pollution. Being more stabile, summer gas is better for storage any time of the year. Fuel stabilizers don't really improve this particular aspect of winter gas.

Various taxes, regulatory measures and subsidized incentives may also be implemented. Towards the end of the Carter administration, domestic oil was reduced to about 1/5th the global price per barrel through various measures including a windfall profit tax on oil companies which basically erased their profit margins from the high global prices keeping oil prices in the US for US use lower than elsewhere in the world. In 1980 the US imported about 40% of the oil it used so this didn't lower the price of gas by 4/5ths, but it did blunt the price felt by consumers. These efforts resulted in the price of gas only doubling instead of quadrupling. If gas prices only doubled from their pre-war average of ~$2.90/gal we'd see ~$5.80/gal.

61950790
>>61950728
>If gas prices only doubled from their pre-war average of ~$2.90/gal we'd see ~$5.80/gal.
a literal nothingburger.

BUYING GAS IS THE CHEAPEST PART OF OWNING A CAR!

61950801
>>61950728
>$8.28/gallon.
I checked and its higher then that in Germany.

61950847
>>61950801
My math was for the US only. As the world's largest producer of oil and now an oil exporting country, we have it better than most other countries.

61950857
>>61949961
No one cares about oil prices anymore, chud.

61950868
>>61950790
Driving a little gasoline Toyota Corolla 100,000 miles @ 40mpg and $5.80/gal: $14,500

Driving a larger electric Toyota bZ4X 100,000 miles at 252 miles per charge and $15/charge: $5,952

Look at what happened to the motor vehicle market after the 1973 and 1979 oil crises. Definitely not a nothingburger for investors.

61950872
>>61950857
Didn't Tesla stop making cars?

61950875
>>61950872
>tesla is the only ev maker
>>61950868
>still not owning an ev

61951080
>>61950184
>non-working radars
Funny that you say that

61951096
>>61949961
What oil stock do I buy bros????

61951236
>>61951080
Here is a real image, unlike your lazy well poisoning attempt
https://edition.cnn.com/2026/03/05/middleeast/radar-bases-us-missile-defense-iran-war-intl-invs

61951283
>>61949961
What will happen on Monday bros..?

61951306
>>61951283
Oil go up

61951325
>>61949961
At this point, over 300. Short term I'm guessing around 150.

61951346
>>61949961
I still don't understand why Americans give a blood sacrifice to Israel every 10 years. Is it some sexual thing?

61951350
>>61949961

This is the biggest oil production cut in the history of mankind. I don’t think an initial spike to $300 before face melting deflation kicks in is out of the question

61951441
>>61951346
It is a sexual thing and it's on tape.

61952245
>>61950875
>still not owning an ev

In the 90s and 2000s my dad was 100% against cell phones, despite cell phones having been around in some form since the 70s. He grudgingly finally got one for business around 2010. Now he doesn't have a home landline. You'll huff and puff for another decade while the world moves on from internal combustion vehicles, but you'll eventually own one like everyone else. Every oil crisis has accelerated the replacement of less efficient vehicles with more efficient vehicles, and we've maxxed out the efficiency of gasoline engines. New battery technology is already doubling range and longevity while halving charge time over what was possible just a couple of years ago. People are running out of excuses to avoid EVs and reasons to keep ICEs. So, you can be the modern equivalent of a guy in the 1930s still riding a horse into town and rejecting those new fangled automobiles, but from an investor standpoint EVs and the technology surrounding them are where the growth is and will continue to be. Personally, I look forward to a world without jeet-owned gas stations and white trash greasemonkey youtube slop.

61952254
>>61951441

oh vey

61952315
>>61952245
Yeah you want overpriced golf carts with planned obsolescence instead. Such an improvement!

61952559
>>61950713
>>61950728
Based and rare high analysis anon, Im doing an investigation currently myself but it’s difficult without drawing from past source’s because the current MSM story is far removed from reality even from a cursory glance. Need to keep eyes on the situation as it unravels, but it seems you’re indicating that this is more than a short spike and could be a year long event?

61954746
>>61950868
Spend an extra $20,000 on a car to save $8548 on gasoline

yore a geneos dude

61955625
>>61949961
it was about -$40 a few years ago so I'm guessing it might reach $3.50 soon

61956311
>>61954746
The nice thing about gasoline cars is that you can "recharge" in less than 5 minutes AND have the ability to double your range simply by carrying extra gas cans.

Electric takes HOURS to charge, and you can't exactly take an extra battery with you.

It's a matter of convenience, and then there is the big brother IoT tracking your every move, which electric vehicles will surely have in them + hardwired kill switches. But that's getting a little off in the weeds.

Combustion engine vehicles are just simply better, more reliable, more convenient.

61956709
slurp the dip?

61957060
>>61952559
>but it seems you’re indicating that this is more than a short spike and could be a year long event?

1. Oil extraction is already shutting down or gas been shut down in the regions that ship oil through the strait.

2. Oil storage in these regions is maxxed out or about to be maxxed out.

3. It takes a couple of weeks to get production going again.

4. It takes a few weeks to queue up tankers, fill them, have them travel halfway around the world, and offload their oil.

5. It takes maybe a week to refine that offloaded oil and have it ready for distribution.

6. Countries like Japan are already tapping their strategic reserves, which will divert some of that oil.

So, if the war ends tomorrow and speculators leave the oil market, fuel prices will still remain elevated due to simple supply constraints for *at least* 6 to 8 weeks. Realistically, the war is going to last at least that long and the strategic reserves of various countries will need even more refilling. We're probably looking at 6 months of high gas prices *at least*.

7. Unlike 1973, the Saudis and a couple of other countries aren't cutting production but increasing it. So prices will likely stabilize at an elevated level soon(ish).

8. Unlike 1979, the US is now a net exporter of oil and oil extraction has increased around the world thanks to new technologies. There are many more vehicles in the world, but the global motor pool is also significantly more efficient than in 1979/80.

So, high fuel prices are going to last awhile, but probably won't get as high as they would if we were facing the exact same situation as in '73 or '79.

61957226
>>61956311
So you drive 300 miles to work or something?

61957526
>>61950050
Then the sellers will just stop shipping and production.
It will go over $200.

item.title: Certified Banger Money Farming — 15 replies — 60.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-09 12:24 UTC

OP

Side hustles, beer money, whatever you wanna call it, just name some good grinds out there that you could recommend to basically anyone
>Uhhhh just invest or hedge against some shitcoin
Not the point of the thread fuck off!
Mostly online methods preferred but not necessary (Yes I am aware some things take physical effort)
If it can be done both on a phone and PC even better
Personally I have been trying to form my own hustle for a little over a month now and have a subscriber base totaling $100 a month, not bad but nothing major

>>61952873

item.title: Iran won't diddle the strait of Hormuz with naval mines, right? — 15 replies — 43.7/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-09 12:17 UTC

OP

Iran won't diddle the strait of Hormuz with naval mines, right? America has only 3 SDF vessels in service...
https://www.chosun.com/english/world-en/2026/03/04/GSFYBQLNMNCJHJVQO5HYIOOACY/

Early replies (first 3)

61955514
hehe thanks for the tip dumb infidel

61955523
>>61954926
you need a real ship to mine. checkout WW2, the germans laid a bunch of joke mines before every uboat lane was filled with so many mines they couldn't leave.

61955540

Modified Shahed to drop naval mines in the sea.
Or heck, to become the mines themselves

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61955549
>>61955523
NO YOU DO NOT. YOU CAN MINE THE STRAIT WITH FUCKING SPEED BOATS. WHERE THE FUCK ARE YOU GETTING THIS INFO?

61955581
WHO THE FUCK IS TELLING PEOPLE THAT THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ IS NO BIG DEAL? I KEEP ENCOUNTERING PEOPLE ON HERE THAT THINK IRAN CAN'T STOP SHIPS FROM GOING THROUGH. THAT THINK IRAN CAN'T JUST MINE THE FUCK OUT OF IT WITH SPEED BOATS OR SEND MISSILES/DRONES/WHATEVER AT TANKERS. THEY ABSOLUTELY CAN AND WILL DO ALL OF THAT. IT'S THE BIGGEST SINGLE RISK WITH ATTACKING IRAN, WE'VE ALWAYS KNOWN THIS WHICH IS WHY WE DIDN'T DO IT FOR DECADES. THERE IS NO SOLUTION. WE COULDN'T STOP FUCKING EVEN THE FUCKING HOUTHIS FROM CLOSING RED SEA TRAFFIC NO MATTER HOW MUCH WE BOMBED THEM

61955591
What are they going to mine it with, drones? The strait is full of american navy vessels. They should have done it before the war started.

61955764
Coast guard sweeps for mines during any naval missions they are the most elite team the actual tip of the spear. It would be very dumb for Iran to engage them. But they will like get engaged anyway.

61955840
>>61954926
Iran is losing big time, don't worry about it

61955891

61956271

>Loses all strategic partners
It would be suicide to do.

61956299

>>61955591

61957460

61957478

item.title: Trump actually got its cards right this time — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Trump actually got its cards right this time

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-03-09 12:27 UTC

OP

America increased its oil exports to their allies while the price is surging to $200 and might even go above $450 at all-time high depending on how long the war drags on. WE WON!

Early replies (first 3)

61957045
>>61957039
RETARD TRANNY detected
Oil is going to $1200

61957076
>>61957039
America only won, because they now control all the oil producers or have capped them with embargos and/or war.

61957097
>>61957039
Only oil companies will win, the local american consumer will not get that oil.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61957112
They'll unveil new tech to make oil far less significant. After they sell the top.

61957117
>>61957097
The common people still benefits from this. The more money and budget the government has, the more we enjoy cheap healthcare and food. Also, it's not going to be us who will be affected from the increasing oil prices but our exporters. There's a reason why Trump made tariffs, and now we're reaping its massive benefits.

61957121
>>61957039
>$200
this will trigger a financial depression
>$450
this will trigger a cataclysm

61957138
>>61957121
>this will trigger a financial depression
Just a few select countries but mostly from enemy countries who are members of BRIC. Countries who are in friendly terms with the US won't largely be affected.

>this will trigger a cataclysm
A civil war, but those who side with the terrorists will likely sink.

61957172
>>61957117
>The more money and budget the government has, the more we enjoy cheap healthcare and food
made it too obvious here

61957501
>>61957076
Reserves =/= production
Case in point is Venezuela, which has huge reserves on paper yet is responsible for less than 1% of global production. Just because oil is technically possible to extract does not mean it is profitable to extract.

i say this as a europoor but i think we have a better shot than the US, but only really because i think the US is so incredibly badly mismanaged right now. biden admin could have probably done the same broad strokes as trump but accomplished them much more competently.

u guys will probably hate the comparison but it looks like ww2 pre-war german/italian shenanigans except now it's US that's impossible to work with due to retarded leadership. if you're a random country like canada or whatever, you're probably more likely to want to integrate more with EU than the US or china at this point. it's clear US has given up on soft power and is trying to just strong-arm countries directly (which doesn't really work if you're trying to do it to everyone at once)

>unless everyone plans a coordinated attack on the US dollar
pretty sure china is basically already trying to by not buying bonds anymore but idk what exactly it would take (getting rid of the petro dollar?)

61957307

61957310
>>61954004

61957320

61957361

Buckle up, because this is only gonna get worse.

61957404

They want to destroy the global economy, millions if not billions will starve, civil wars are going to break out everywhere globally.

item.title: The US has 10 days — 160 replies — 205.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 160 • Updated: 2026-03-09 11:51 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61955417
The night before the new 1929 crash

61955547
>>61938341
>10 days
markets are crashing right fucking now

61955763
>>61954125
Tax credits for EVs. Just in time for the oil crisis.

61955846
>>61938353
> I don’t know how the market would view boots on the ground
They'd freak out. Iran is bigger than France, Germany, Spain, and the United Kingdom combined. That's a lot of ground. The US has military officers, who went to war college, who told them this would be a mess.
>>61938390
Cheap drones. The strait at its most narrow point is 33km wide (20 miles). It's not like they'd have a hard time finding the ships passing through there.

61955894
>>61938425
Trump is Denji and Netanyahu is Makima. With this in mind, everything makes sense.

61955957
>>61942764
>muh agi
Absolute meme tech kill yourself

61955996
>>61942837
Obama did that, retard.

61956026
>>61938402
They don't need to be anywhere near the shore to chuck drones, retardo.

61956041
>>61938528
Where do you miggers get your confidence?

Here's a tip, invest in Goyboxes.

61956066
>>61955316
China is Iran's ally.

61956110
>>61938402
Nah you can launch shaheads from the back of a ute, I've seen it done
Also, USVs do not require any launch platform at all and have proven devastatingly effective in the black sea - though with that said I have yet to see Iran or its houthi proxies deploy those in any serious number

61956116
>>61938341
just got into trading, is it supposed to be doing that?

61956309
>>61956116
yes. it's normal.

61956552
>>61956041
>meanwhile in Iran

61956572
>>61938341
two more weeks

61956577
how much time would you wager a release of circa 400 million barrels from the G7 would buy?

61956580
>>61956577
Consumption is ~100m/day desu

61956581
>>61956577
If it is just meant to replace average Hormuz traffic (20 million barrels a day), that's 20 days?

61956822
>>61953413
>that's a death sentence since you can't shoplift gas
Not with that attitude
>>61954112
It'll probably be on it anyways

61957005
did someone capitulated? why the dip?

61957018
>>61938341
>not a hyperbole
ESL schizophrenic #9000 making predictions like he knows anything

back to your rice farm buddy.

61957024
>>61957005

why can't any of you speak english?

where do they find you illiterate faggots?

61957027
>>61957024
at ur mom

61957036
>>61957027
bloody benchod your sister you basterd

61957048
>>61957018

You don’t know what a hyperbole is? You drop out of high school?

61957128
>>61957036
I stand redeemed.

61957134
>>61957048
It's
>hyperbole
not
>A hyperbole

you say "a hyperbole" and everyone knows you're from india

61957139
>>61956041

61957381
>>61942837
>il price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget
the us gov does not get anything from oil prices going up kek. Slightly higher taxs from oil companies but not in any siginificant amount.

61957411
>>61957134

I’m not from India I literally have a masters degree from an Ivy League. I don’t give a fuck what some loser thinks. Post your longs or get out of my thread. We already know you got burned on your weekend CL shorts

item.title: WFH CHADS REPORT IN — 23 replies — 27.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
WFH CHADS REPORT IN

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-03-09 11:48 UTC

OP

Let's all laugh at the wagies who have to pay insane amounts for gas to get to work kek

Early replies (first 3)

61953886
>>61953855
Imagine spending half your days pay worth of money just to get to work and back lmao

61954053
>>61953855
antisemitic wfh antizog antimiga chads will inherit the earth

61954077
>>61953886
>half
You mean full for minimum wage chads, don’t forget about rent and bills too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61954144
>>61953855
that reminds me, anon, I need to get my car inspected and oil changed (first time in a year kek) before the end of the month. I forget I own a car sometimes living downtown and WFH, thanks!

61954162
>>61953855
starting week two of my new wfh job. any recommendations for a mouse jiggler?

61954234
I WFH on MWF. Tuesdays and Wednesdays are fucking hell for me. It takes me minimum 3 hours to and back to commute and its only like 35km. Plus $15 in fares and and $13-20 lunch.

61954249
>>61954234
microwave sardines in the break room and leave them there to rot

61954252
>>61953855
>for gas to get to work
I live 2 streets away from the office kek I even go home to eat lunch sometimes.

61954356
>>61953855
Im currently clocked in on the job right now. Sundays is essentially my monday where i clean my apartment (rent is 780 per month). I also live a block away from corporate headquarters (even though I never go into the office). The thing is that I only go there for the 24/7 open snacks and sodas at 4am. During the daytime though before my shift starts at 3pm, i do my daily workout routine at the company provided gym 6 days a week. After doing my weightlifting routine and showering at the company spa, I treat myself to a double patty burger, bacon, cheese and 2 full cups of the soup of the day for a total of 6 dollars.

61954489
I WFH.

There was a very kino moment on Saturday morning where I woke up earlier than usual, it was sunny for the first time in weeks, I went jogging, did a few chores, then I drove to McDonald's at 10 am for a bacon and egg mcmuffin (never had McDonald's breakfast before) and a black coffee and it all felt so kino. It felt like being unemployed again.

61954507
>>61954234
Make your own lunch you lazy faggot

61954577
>>61954356
I also forgot to mention that the corporate office has a fully staffed kitchen team that cooks anything you request. If you want an omlette at 6pm, you can have it. If you want a burger at 8am, there you go. Including daily desserts and a soft serve machine next to the salad bar. (Sorry but i still wont sit in the cubicle. I refuse to do so!)

61954585
>>61953855
I'm an EVChud (we won btw) but I also work from home.

ICEissies big mad :)

61954808
>>61953855
WfH accountant reporting in
I hate working, need to find a new job in the next couple months because rn my work load is low but it's going to increase a lot

61954827
>>61954162
Build one yourself nigger. Use claude

61954912
>>61954577
Damn how big is that fucking office?
We just get our food paid for by the company (unless it is retardedly expensive, but they cover enough for the usual menu price) and eat at the restaurants around the office which are all pretty good (mediterranean country).
The office itself is very old-school and just has a canteen room for eating. No free stuff of cooking staff. The usual for a boomer era company.

61954933
>>61954808
Get a government job anon. In your field the pay difference isn't even that big afaik and the workload difference is huge.

61954954
I have been applying for WFH jobs with no success. Any advice?

I haven't applied for a new job in years and I am amazed at how many fake job postings there are. I had one company that wanted me to sign their t&cs and very an account to get an interview. Looking into it, they were going to our new through multiple technical tests for interviews and then keep my responses to train their AI

61954976
>>61954954
Oh and I have avoided Social media all these years and now finally caved and got LinkedIn because job listings actually want you to list your LinkedIn account on your application. Not sure if it'll actually help at all

61954991
>>61954912
Pretty big, the weightlifting room at our facility is comparable to an average high school basketball gym in size.

61955153
>>61954933
>Get a government job anon
I want one but none in my county have WfH, I mean I haven't gotten an interview with one yet so I'd have to ask

61957384
>have regular 9-5
>wake up an hour earlier for a work event
>6am
>out the window
>still night time
>traffic in full swing
There is something very wrong about this. The natural world is brutal but this is the same thing just in slow-mo suffering mode.

61957401
>>61953855
Bros, I am officially celebrating six years of getting away with WFH. Thanks Wuhan!

item.title: how tf do you buy oil? Is there like an amazon site where you buy barrells of oil? WTF? — 15 replies — 16.4/hr

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-09 11:43 UTC

OP

how tf do you buy oil? Is there like an amazon site where you buy barrells of oil? WTF?

Early replies (first 3)

61955665
you just go to the gas station

61955686
You can't buy oil. Strait's closed

61955807

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61955855
Americans say gas but in fact they don't mean gas they mean gasoline but they are just too lazy to say the entire word "gasoline" so they say gas.

61955950

61955956

61956192

61956253

61957302
Oh, you don't have to buy it! Open your mouth... HHHHHNGGHHGGUH, oh yeah, oh yeah here's some beautiful oil for you!

61957305

61957308

item.title: OIL SHORT SQUEEZE TO $1000 — 12 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
OIL SHORT SQUEEZE TO $1000

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-09 11:32 UTC

OP

Oil could squeeze to over $1000 if these shorts by the Treasury Department and Israel-friendly banks (all of them lol) go the wrong way. They're using massive oil futures shorts to suppress the price in a time when the supply is already extremely constrained, which is insane. There may not even be enough oil to cover their shorts if they have to close them. Spread the word.

https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/us-weighs-oil-futures-market-action-combat-rising-energy-prices-wh-official-2026-03-05/
>inb4 >Reuters
It's the source with the most detailed description of their plan to short oil futures.

Early replies (first 3)

61956951
>>61956946
sensible chuckle

61956960
>short squeeze the US government
like >>61956951 said, amusing but they'll just double down and say no in fulfilling the contacts. national security ;^)

61956974
>>61956960
That would break the oil market and have almost the same effect. They don't and can't control the oil market for the entire planet.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956975
>>61956946
Ok now explain it to me like im 5

61956987
>>61956975
The government sells negative oil (lol) to suppress the price because oil production is being disrupted while us amerigoys die for israel in the middle east, but the government will eventually have to purchase positive (real) oil to close their short positions, in a time when there may not be enough physical oil to buy in the first place because all of the supply disruptions. As the government has to buy scarce physical oil to stop the losses on their shorts, it will push the oil price even higher, causing them to lose more money purchase more oil at higher and higher prices in a feedback loop. The end result is oil surpassing $1000 a barrel.

61956991
>>61956946
>$1000
kys

61956994
>>61956987
>https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/us-weighs-oil-futures-market-action-combat-rising-energy-prices-wh-official-2026-03-05/
Ok thanks btw the article you posted requires people to pay for a subscription to read it

61957007
>>61956991
Cope.

>>61956994
It literally doesn't, you made that up.

61957143
>>61957007
It does. I would like to read it, but it is paywalled.

61957148
>>61956946
i cant tell if this is a troll post if youre literally retarded

61957237
>>61957007

61957346
>>61957143
>You’re a moron. It is not paywalled.
Jew banks to tamp oil like gold and silver, what could go wrong?!
